channel.title: Home - ChessBase India
channel.description: Read every news about chess in detail with ChessBase India in English. Read English news related with Chess such as Tournament, Players, every report of the Match.

item.title: Goutham Krishna H clear first at 35th RCC STS Blitz Rating 2026
item.description: IM Goutham Krishna H played brilliant chess to score 10.5/11 points to win the 35th RCC STS International FIDE Rated Blitz Chess Cup 2026. After 11 rounds of play, Goutham was clear 1.5 points ahead of the field, while 13 players scored 9/11 points to secure 2nd to 14th places. IM CRG Krishna and IM Nitin S secured 2nd and 3rd place, respectively, according to tiebreak scores. The tournament was organized by Rao’s Chess Corner at St. Theresas School, Bethany Institution, Bendur in Mangalore, Karnataka on 29th March 2026. The total prize fund was ₹1,00,000 with top 3 prizes of ₹20,000, ₹10,000, and ₹7,000 respectively. Photos: Rao's Chess Corner

item.title: V S Nandish and Pratitee Bordoloi are the Indian Under-17 National Champions
item.description: V S Nandish of Tamil Nadu and Pratitee Bordoloi of Karnataka won the 35th National U-17 Open and Girls Chess Championship 2026. In the Open section, FM V S Nandish (TN) scored 9.5/11 points to clinch the National Championship, and Ethan Vaz (Goa) scored 9/11 to secure 2nd place. 7 players scored 8.5/11 to finish from 3rd to 9th place, respectively; Madhvendra Pratap Sharma (MP) secured 3rd place on better tiebreak scores. In the Girls section, Pratitee Bordoloi scored 9/11 to become National Champion, Saranya Devi Narahari (AP) scored 8.5/11 to secure 2nd place, and Anushka Bapu Kutwal (MAH) secured 3rd place after scoring 8/11. The National Championship was organised by the Gujarat State Chess Association in Ahmedabad. Photos: Neelkanth Kagathara

item.title: Invitation to Foreign Grandmasters to play at 360 One Global Chess Festival in Mumbai
item.description: The 360 One Global Chess Festival returns with its 9th edition, to be held in the vibrant city of Mumbai from 10th to 18th June 2026. Over the years, this tournament has built a strong legacy as one of India’s premier international chess events, consistently attracting top talent and providing a competitive platform for rising stars and seasoned professionals alike. This year promises to be stronger than ever. The Masters section has been carefully curated to ensure a high-quality field, with entry for Indian players restricted to those who have crossed a classical rating of 2400 in the last two years (July 2024 to June 2026 FRL).

item.title: Mihir Shah convincingly wins 2025-26 Grand Prix at Phoenix ChessBase India Club
item.description: Mihir Shah displayed a dominant performance throughout the year at Phoenix ChessBase India Chess Club in Mumbai. 12 monthly blitz tournaments were held on the 4th Saturday of every month at the club, and the winners secured Cash prizes, ChessBase India gift hampers, as well as Grand Prix points. Mihir participated in all 12 events, starting from April 2025, and won 7/12 tournaments. The Finale was conducted on 4th April with the higher Stakes as the Grand Prix points were double, but Mihir showed his consistency and won the finale too, and finished on 289 points. FM Anirudhha Potawad scored 153 points and secured 2nd place, while Iyer Arvind scored 105 to secure 3rd place. Photos: Nehal Deshmukh, Aanchal Dhakar/ChessBase India

item.title: Abhimanyu Puranik wins ₹1,00,000 after dominating at 3rd JKs ECA Rapid Rating 2026
item.description: Abhimanyu Puranik scored an unbeaten 8.5/9 points to win the 3rd JKs ECA All India Open FIDE Rapid Rating Chess Tournament 2026. Abhimanyu drew against Akshay Borgaonkar in the 7th round and won his remaining games. Karthikeyan P, Vaibhav Jayant Raut, and Shlok Sharnarthi scored 8/9 points to finish in 2nd-4th place according to tiebreak scores. The tournament was conducted by JK's Excellence Chess Academy on 19th April at Gulmohor Banquets, Abhiruchi Mall, Pune. The total prize fund was ₹4,00,000 with top 3 prizes of ₹1,00,000, ₹50,000, and ₹25,000, respectively. Photos: Ravindra Potawad/ChessBase India

item.title: Viani Antonio Dcunha wins 34th RCC STS Rapid Rating 2026
item.description: IM Viani Antonio Dcunha scored an unbeaten 9/10 points to win the 34th RCC STS International FIDE Rated Rapid Chess Cup 2026. Viani Antonio Dcunha, Goutham Krishna H, and Harsh Suresh scored 9/10 points and secured 1st to 3rd places respectively on tiebreak scores. The tournament was organized by Rao’s Chess Corner at St. Theresas School, Bethany Institution, Bendur in Mangalore, Karnataka, from 28th March to 29th March 2026. The total prize fund was ₹2,00,000, with top 3 prizes of ₹40,000, ₹25,000, and ₹12,500 along with trophies. Photos: Rao's Chess Corner

item.title: Jaiveer Mahendru perfect at Children's Research University Under 18 Rapid Rating 2026
item.description: FM Jaiveer Mahendru scored a perfect 9/9 points to win the Children’s Research University All India Open FIDE Rated Rapid Under-18 Chess Tournament-2026. 10 players scored 8/9 points to finish from 2nd to 11th place; better tiebreak scores helped Viresh Sharnarthi (MAH) and Haridev S (TN) to secure 2nd and 3rd place. The tournament was organised by CR University at the Veer Savarkar Sports Complex in Ahmedabad, on 28th March 2026. The total prize fund of this One Day Rapid Rating event was ₹4,00,000, with top 3 prizes of ₹25,000, ₹21,000, and ₹14,000. Photos: IA Prof R. Anantharam

item.title: Nitin S wins 33rd RCC STS Classical Rating 2026
item.description: IM Nitin S scored an unbeaten 8/9 points to win the 33rd RCC STS International FIDE Rated Classical Chess Cup 2026. Nitin S and Sham R both scored 8/9 points to finish in 1st and 2nd place according to tiebreak scores. Sarvana Krishnan P, Murali Krishnan BT, Nishant TR, and Anilkumar OT scored 7.5/9 points to finish in 3rd to 6th places, respectively. The total prize fund of the tournament was ₹5,00,001, and the top 3 prizes were ₹70,000, ₹50,000, and ₹35,000. The tournament was organized by Rao’s Chess Corner at St. Theresas School, Bethany Institution, Bendur in Mangalore, Karnataka, from 27th March to 1st April 2026. Photos: Rao's Chess Corner

item.title: R Vaishali secures the World Championship Spot
item.description: It's been almost 5 days since the FIDE Candidates 2026 was over, and we have two challengers for the World Championship Title. Vaishali Rameshbabu won her last game against Kateryna Lagno to win the Women's Candidates. Javokhir Sindarov was unstoppable from the start and won the Candidates with a round to spare, remaining unbeaten throughout the event. Vaishali will play the World Championship match against Ju Wenjun, and Javokhir Sindarov will take on D Gukesh for the title. Check out the prize distribution ceremony photos in this article. Photos: Michal Walusza, Niki Riga/ FIDE

item.title: Storytime with Viswanathan Anand and Aruna Anand
item.description: What if one story could change a child’s life? Across India, many children are still waiting to discover the joy of reading. The Turning Pages Foundation is working to change that through its India Reads Aloud initiative. They collaborate with famous personalities to capture a child’s imagination through storytelling. In a recent episode, Viswanathan Anand and Aruna Anand joined this initiative by narrating a story for young listeners. Read on to discover how this idea is creating a lasting impact. Photos: Turning Pages Foundation

item.title: The Real Battle Begins: Gukesh vs Sindarov -Who Has the Edge?
item.description: Right now, if there is one player dominating the global chess conversation, it is Uzbekistan’s 20-year-old Javokhir Sindarov. After winning the FIDE Candidates in brilliant style, he earned the right to fight for the world title against reigning World Champion Gukesh. In this article, we explore how he claimed the Candidates title and assess his upcoming World Championship clash with Gukesh. Photo: Maria Emelianova & Michal Walusza / FIDE

item.title: 10 unknown photos of R. Vaishali
item.description: The whole world knows that Vaishali Rameshbabu won the FIDE Candidates 2026, and now she is going to play the World Championship match against reigning Women's World Chess Champion Ju Wenjun. The stories of the candidates and Vaishali's journey are all over the internet. But what if we bring you 10 unseen special photos of Vaishali's Career? Check out this short article with her photos and the backstory behind each one of them.

item.title: Javokhir Sindarov on preparing to face D Gukesh for the World title
item.description: The chess world is witnessing a generational shift. We are seeing new names emerge as contenders for the World title. After winning the FIDE Candidates Tournament 2026, Uzbek star Javokhir Sindarov has emerged as the new challenger for the World Championship. In a recent interview after his victory, Sindarov reflected on his journey, key moments, and preparation. He also shared his thoughts on facing World Champion Gukesh in what promises to be a historic clash. Read the article to know more. Photo: Michal Walusza

item.title: Javokhir Sindarov's secret weapon - his coach IM Roman Vidonyak
item.description: Javokhir Sindarov had an incredible performance at the FIDE Candidates 2026. He won the tournament by a margin of 1.5 points over the field. While a lot of people have contributed towards Javokhir's success - one man takes the lion's share. His name IM Roman Vidonyak and he is the coach of Sindarov. The journey of Roman and Javokhir began in January 2025 and in the last 1 year and 4 months, they have managed to achieve something truly special. What are Roman's methods of training? We tried to understand from the man himself.

item.title: Vincent Keymer wins Grenke Freestyle 2026, Harika qualifies for FIDE Freestyle World Championship
item.description: Vincent Keymer drew his last game against Maxime Vachier Lagrave to win Grenke Freestyle 2026. Both players were co-leading the event going into the last round, and Vincent had a better tie-break score, which helped him win the title and spot in the Freestyle World Championship 2027. He scored 7.5/9 points, starting with an insane 6 wins in a row and drawing against Magnus Carlsen, Alexey Sarana, and Maxime Vachier-Lagrave in the last 3 rounds. In the women’s standings, Harika Dronavalli, Alua Nurman, and Dinara Wagner secured 3 spots for the upcoming Freestyle World Championship 2027. It was a great event for Indian players as Aryan drew his last game against Carlsen to finish in the top 10 with Leon Mendonca. Photos: Abhyudaya Ram/Prajwal Bhat
